title: Indoor Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The indoor ambient temperature sensor has malfunctioned or is sending incorrect readings to the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code E1 on Samsung air conditioners indicates a failure in the indoor room temperature sensor (thermistor). This sensor measures the ambient air temperature in the room and sends data to the control board so the unit can regulate cooling or heating output. When the sensor fails or its readings drift outside the expected range, the unit displays E1 and may stop operating.

The most common cause is a faulty thermistor that has degraded over time due to heat exposure. A loose or corroded wire connection between the sensor and the indoor PCB can also trigger this error. In rare cases, the main indoor control board itself may be defective.

Before replacing parts, try a power reset by turning off the breaker for 30 seconds. If the error persists, the thermistor should be tested with a multimeter and replaced if its resistance is outside the manufacturer's specification.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded wiring between sensor and PCB

symptoms: E1 error displayed on indoor unit panel or remote
Unit stops cooling or heating
Fan may continue running but compressor does not engage
Inconsistent temperature regulation before error appears

fixSteps.title: Power reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the air conditioner at the circuit breaker for 30 seconds, then turn it back on. This clears transient sensor faults.

fixSteps.title: Access the indoor unit
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel of the indoor unit by lifting the clips on each side. Locate the indoor temperature sensor — a small thermistor usually clipped to the evaporator coil area or mounted near the air intake.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wire harness connecting the thermistor to the indoor PCB. Ensure the connector is fully seated and there is no corrosion or damage to the wires.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure its resistance with a multimeter. At 25°C (77°F) it should read approximately 10 kOhms. If the reading is open, shorted, or significantly out of range, replace the thermistor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Order a replacement thermistor matching your model number. Unclip the old sensor, disconnect it from the PCB connector, and install the new one in the same position.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor tests normal but the error persists, the indoor PCB may need replacement, which requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E1 error fix

faq.q: Can I still use my Samsung AC with E1 error?
faq.a: The unit may not cool or heat properly with this error. It is best to resolve it before continued use to avoid further damage.

faq.q: How much does a replacement thermistor cost?
faq.a: Samsung AC thermistors typically cost $5–$15. Professional installation may add $100–$200 in labor.

title: Indoor Coil Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The indoor evaporator coil temperature sensor has failed or is reading abnormal values.
fullDescription:
Error code E2 indicates a problem with the indoor evaporator coil temperature sensor (pipe thermistor). This sensor monitors the temperature of the evaporator coil to prevent freezing and to manage defrost cycles. When it fails, the control board cannot properly regulate compressor operation.

A faulty pipe thermistor is the most frequent cause. The sensor may have degraded due to prolonged exposure to temperature extremes. Wiring issues between the sensor and the indoor PCB, or a buildup of ice on the coil blocking the sensor, can also trigger E2.

Start by checking if the coil is heavily iced over, which could indicate an airflow problem rather than a sensor issue. If the coil is clear, test the thermistor resistance and replace if out of specification.

causes.cause: Ice buildup on evaporator coil interfering with sensor
Wiring damage between sensor and PCB
Dirty air filter restricting airflow causing coil freeze

symptoms: E2 error code on display
Unit shuts down or runs continuously without proper cooling
Ice formation on the indoor evaporator coil
Water leaking from the indoor unit due to ice melting

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit at the breaker for 30 seconds. Allow any ice on the coil to melt (may take 1–2 hours if heavily iced).

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the air filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the air filter under running water. A clogged filter restricts airflow and causes coil freezing, which can trigger E2.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the coil sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel and locate the pipe thermistor clipped to the evaporator coil. Check for physical damage, loose clips, or disconnected wires.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the thermistor resistance with a multimeter. It should read about 10 kOhms at 25°C. If out of range, replace with a matching part.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring are fine but the error continues, the indoor PCB may be faulty. A technician should diagnose the board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E2 error fix

faq.q: Why does my Samsung AC keep freezing up?
faq.a: A clogged air filter, low refrigerant, or a faulty coil sensor can all cause the evaporator to freeze. Clean the filter first, then check the sensor.

faq.q: Is E2 the same as E1?
faq.a: No. E1 is the room temperature sensor while E2 is the coil temperature sensor. They are different thermistors in different locations.

title: Outdoor Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The outdoor ambient temperature sensor is malfunctioning or disconnected.
fullDescription:
Error code E3 signals a fault with the outdoor unit's ambient temperature sensor. This thermistor measures the outside air temperature to help the control system optimize compressor speed and refrigerant flow. When it fails, the system cannot adjust properly for outdoor conditions.

The outdoor sensor is exposed to weather, UV radiation, and temperature extremes, making it more prone to degradation than indoor sensors. Rodent damage to wiring is also common in outdoor units.

A power reset may temporarily clear the error, but if the thermistor has failed, it will need to be replaced. The outdoor unit must be accessed, which may require professional help if mounted in a difficult location.

causes.cause: Rodent or weather damage to sensor wiring

symptoms: E3 error on indoor display or remote
Compressor may not start or runs erratically
Unit fails to adjust output for outdoor temperature changes
System operates in a fixed-speed fallback mode

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for the outdoor unit for 30 seconds, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Inspect outdoor sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the outdoor unit cover and locate the ambient temperature sensor, typically mounted near the coil inlet. Check wires for rodent damage, corrosion, or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test resistance with a multimeter. At 25°C it should read approximately 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a replacement outdoor ambient thermistor matching your model. Route the wiring away from any sharp edges and secure with cable ties.

whenToCallPro: If the outdoor unit is roof-mounted or otherwise hard to access, or if the outdoor PCB needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E3 error fix

faq.q: Can outdoor weather cause E3 errors?
faq.a: Extreme cold or heat alone should not trigger E3. The error indicates a sensor malfunction, not just extreme readings.

faq.q: Is the outdoor sensor the same part as the indoor sensor?
faq.a: They may have different part numbers and resistance values. Always check your model's parts list.

title: Outdoor Coil Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The outdoor condenser coil temperature sensor has failed or is reading abnormal values.
fullDescription:
Error code E4 indicates a fault with the outdoor condenser coil temperature sensor (pipe thermistor). This sensor monitors the condenser coil temperature to manage defrost cycles in heating mode and to protect the compressor from overheating in cooling mode.

When this sensor fails, the system may not defrost the outdoor coil properly in winter, leading to ice buildup and reduced efficiency. In summer, it may cause the compressor to shut down prematurely as the control board cannot verify coil temperatures.

The sensor is mounted directly on the outdoor coil and is exposed to the elements. Corrosion and thermal fatigue are common failure modes.

causes.cause: Sensor displaced from coil contact point

symptoms: E4 error code displayed
Outdoor coil ices up in heating mode
Compressor shuts down prematurely in cooling mode
Defrost cycle does not activate when needed

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Switch off the outdoor unit breaker for 30 seconds, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor positioning
fixSteps.instruction: Open the outdoor unit panel and locate the pipe thermistor clipped to the condenser coil. Make sure it is firmly attached to the coil surface and has not slipped out of its clip.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and test resistance. At 25°C it should be around 10 kOhms. Replace if out of spec.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install the new thermistor in the same position on the coil. Ensure good thermal contact and secure the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the outdoor coil is heavily iced and you suspect a refrigerant issue rather than a sensor issue, call a licensed HVAC technician.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E4 error fix

faq.q: Can low refrigerant cause E4?
faq.a: Low refrigerant causes abnormal coil temperatures, but E4 specifically indicates the sensor itself is faulty. However, a technician should check refrigerant levels if the sensor tests normal.

faq.q: My outdoor unit is covered in ice. Is that E4?
faq.a: Ice buildup can be a symptom of E4 because the defrost cycle cannot activate without accurate coil temperature readings.

title: Compressor Overload / Overcurrent Error
shortDescription: The compressor is drawing excessive current, triggering the overcurrent protection circuit.
fullDescription:
Error code E5 indicates that the compressor is drawing more current than the rated limit. The overcurrent protection has tripped to prevent damage to the compressor and electrical components. This is a serious error that should be addressed promptly.

Common causes include a locked compressor rotor, low or overcharged refrigerant, high outdoor ambient temperature combined with dirty condenser coils, or electrical issues such as low supply voltage. A failing compressor that has internal short circuits will also draw excessive current.

This error often requires professional diagnosis because refrigerant system issues and compressor faults are involved. However, basic checks like cleaning the condenser coil and verifying voltage can be done by a homeowner.

causes.cause: Dirty condenser coil causing high head pressure
Blocked or restricted airflow around outdoor unit

symptoms: E5 error code displayed
Compressor stops running
Outdoor unit may make a humming or clicking sound
Circuit breaker may trip
Unit restarts and stops repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser coil
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit. Use a garden hose to gently rinse dirt, leaves, and debris from the outdoor condenser coil fins. Spray from inside out if possible.

fixSteps.title: Ensure adequate airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Clear any obstructions within 60 cm (2 feet) of the outdoor unit on all sides. Remove plants, debris, or covers blocking airflow.

fixSteps.title: Check supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, verify the voltage at the outdoor disconnect. It should be within 10% of the rated voltage (typically 220-240V). Low voltage causes the compressor to draw excess current.

fixSteps.title: Call a professional
fixSteps.instruction: If basic checks do not resolve the issue, a technician needs to check refrigerant charge, compressor winding resistance, and the inverter board.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the condenser coil and checking voltage does not resolve E5, professional diagnosis is required. Compressor replacement can cost $500–$1500.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E5 error fix

faq.q: Is E5 dangerous?
faq.a: The overcurrent protection prevents fire or compressor damage. However, repeated tripping can degrade the compressor. Have it repaired promptly.

faq.q: Can a dirty filter cause E5?
faq.a: A dirty indoor filter restricts airflow and can indirectly raise system pressures, but E5 is more commonly caused by outdoor coil fouling or refrigerant issues.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor is not running or not reaching the expected speed.
fullDescription:
Error code E154 indicates that the indoor fan motor has failed to operate correctly. The control board detects that the fan is not spinning or is not reaching the programmed speed within the expected time. The system shuts down to prevent the evaporator from freezing due to lack of airflow.

A stuck or seized fan motor is the most common cause. Dust and debris accumulation on the fan blades can overload the motor over time. A failed motor capacitor (on non-inverter models) or a faulty fan motor control circuit on the indoor PCB can also trigger E154.

Start by checking if the fan blade rotates freely by hand. If it is stuck or stiff, the motor bearings may have worn out.

causes.cause: Dust buildup on fan blades causing motor overload
Indoor PCB fan motor control circuit failure

symptoms: E154 error on display
Indoor unit is silent — no fan sound
Evaporator coil may freeze up
Weak or no airflow from vents

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 30 seconds, then restore power and try again.

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan blades
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel and remove the air filters. With the unit off and unplugged, carefully clean dust and debris from the blower fan (crossflow fan) using a soft brush or vacuum.

fixSteps.title: Check fan rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With the unit off and unplugged, try to rotate the fan blades by hand. They should spin freely. If stiff or seized, the motor bearings are worn and the motor needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wire connector between the fan motor and the indoor PCB. Ensure it is firmly plugged in with no burned or damaged pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is confirmed faulty, order a replacement matching your model. This typically involves removing the blower assembly, which is best done by a technician.

whenToCallPro: Fan motor replacement requires disassembling the indoor unit. If you are not comfortable with this, call a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E154 indoor fan motor error fix

faq.q: Why did my indoor fan stop working?
faq.a: Dust accumulation, worn bearings, or electrical failure in the motor are the most common reasons. Regular cleaning helps prevent this.

faq.q: Can I run the AC without the indoor fan?
faq.a: No. Without airflow the evaporator coil will freeze, and the compressor could be damaged by liquid refrigerant returning through the suction line.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Speed Error
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor is running but not reaching the correct speed as commanded by the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code E163 indicates that while the indoor fan motor is operating, it is not reaching the target RPM set by the control board. The board monitors fan speed via a feedback signal and triggers E163 when the actual speed deviates significantly from the expected speed.

This can be caused by a partially seized motor where bearings are degrading but the motor still turns. Heavy dust accumulation on the fan blades adds resistance and slows the motor. On inverter-type indoor units, the motor driver circuit on the PCB may be weakening.

Unlike E154 where the fan does not run at all, E163 means the fan is running slowly. The unit may still blow some air, but cooling performance will be poor.

causes.cause: Heavy dust buildup on fan blades

symptoms: E163 error code on display
Fan runs but airflow is noticeably weak
Unusual humming or grinding noise from indoor unit
Poor cooling or heating performance

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan blades thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front panel and remove the filters. Use a long-bristle brush, damp cloth, or steamer to clean all dust from the crossflow fan blades. Heavily soiled fans can lose 30-50% airflow.

fixSteps.title: Power reset after cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Restore power and run the unit. If E163 clears and airflow is strong, the issue was dust buildup.

fixSteps.title: Listen for bearing noise
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, listen carefully to the fan motor. A grinding or scraping sound indicates worn bearings that require motor replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If bearings are worn, the motor must be replaced. This requires removing the blower assembly from the indoor unit.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the error, motor replacement requires professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E163 fan speed error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E154 and E163?
faq.a: E154 means the fan is not running at all. E163 means it is running but too slowly. E163 is often fixable by cleaning.

faq.q: How often should I clean the indoor fan?
faq.a: A professional deep clean every 1–2 years is recommended. Clean the filters monthly during heavy use seasons.

title: Abnormal Indoor/Outdoor Communication Error
shortDescription: The indoor and outdoor units are unable to communicate properly over the signal wire.
fullDescription:
Error code E171 indicates a communication failure between the indoor and outdoor units. Samsung air conditioners use a signal wire (typically a 2-wire or 3-wire communication cable) to exchange data between units. When this communication link is disrupted, the system cannot coordinate compressor operation with indoor settings.

Common causes include a damaged or incorrectly wired communication cable, a faulty connector, or a failed PCB on either the indoor or outdoor unit. Power surges from lightning strikes can damage the communication circuits on both boards simultaneously.

This error is particularly common after new installation or after electrical storms. Check the communication wiring first, as this resolves the issue in most cases.

causes.cause: Damaged or loose communication wire between units
PCB communication circuit damage from power surge

symptoms: E171 displayed on indoor panel
Indoor unit runs fan only — no cooling or heating
Outdoor unit does not start
Error appears shortly after power-on

fixSteps.title: Power cycle both units
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker supplying both indoor and outdoor units for 60 seconds, then restore power. Allow 3 minutes for the units to re-establish communication.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the communication cable
fixSteps.instruction: Check the signal wire running from the indoor unit terminal block to the outdoor unit terminal block. Ensure the wire is not damaged, pinched, or exposed. Verify correct terminal connections match the wiring diagram.

fixSteps.title: Check terminal connections
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten all terminal screws on both indoor and outdoor connection blocks. Look for signs of corrosion, arcing, or melted insulation on the terminals.

fixSteps.title: Test communication voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter on AC voltage mode, measure between the signal terminals at the outdoor unit. You should see a fluctuating voltage indicating data communication. No voltage or steady DC suggests a broken line or failed board.

whenToCallPro: If wiring checks are normal but the error persists, one of the PCBs may be damaged and needs professional diagnosis with oscilloscope or board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E171 communication error fix

faq.q: Can a lightning strike cause E171?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the communication circuits on the PCBs. A surge protector is recommended for air conditioning systems.

faq.q: I just installed my Samsung AC and get E171 immediately. What is wrong?
faq.a: Most likely the communication wire is connected to the wrong terminals. Double-check the wiring diagram in the installation manual.

title: Indoor/Outdoor Unit Communication Timeout
shortDescription: The indoor unit has not received a response from the outdoor unit within the expected time window.
fullDescription:
Error code E186 is a communication timeout error. Unlike E171 which indicates a general communication failure, E186 specifically means the indoor unit sent a command to the outdoor unit but did not receive a response within the allowed time. This points to intermittent communication issues rather than a complete break.

Intermittent connections are often caused by a loose wire that makes contact some of the time, electromagnetic interference from nearby high-voltage lines, or a degrading PCB component that works intermittently. The error may appear and disappear on its own.

Since this is intermittent, it can be harder to diagnose. Start by checking all connections and ensuring the communication wire is separated from power cables to minimize interference.

causes.cause: Intermittent loose connection in communication wiring
Electromagnetic interference from nearby power lines
Long cable run with insufficient wire gauge

symptoms: E186 appears intermittently
Unit works sometimes but stops randomly
Outdoor unit may start and stop frequently
Error clears on its own and returns later

fixSteps.title: Tighten all connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power. Open both indoor and outdoor terminal blocks and tighten all screw terminals. Even a quarter turn can fix an intermittent contact.

fixSteps.title: Separate signal and power wires
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the communication cable is not bundled together with the power supply cables. Electromagnetic interference from power wires can corrupt the signal. Separate them by at least 15 cm.

fixSteps.title: Replace the communication cable
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is old, damaged, or undersized, replace it with new shielded cable of adequate gauge (1.5 mm² minimum for runs up to 15 meters).

fixSteps.title: Professional PCB check
fixSteps.instruction: If the issue persists with new wiring, the outdoor PCB communication circuitry may need to be tested and possibly replaced.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the communication cable does not stop the intermittent errors, professional PCB diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E186 communication timeout error fix

faq.q: Why does E186 come and go?
faq.a: E186 is a timeout error, meaning communication works sometimes but fails intermittently. A loose connection or interference is the usual culprit.

faq.q: What is the difference between E171 and E186?
faq.a: E171 is a complete communication failure. E186 is a timeout, meaning communication is intermittent.

title: Compressor Overcurrent Protection Activated
shortDescription: The compressor current has exceeded the safety threshold and the overcurrent protection relay has tripped.
fullDescription:
Error code E201 indicates that the compressor overcurrent protection has been activated at the outdoor unit level. This is a hardware-level protection that trips when the compressor draws more current than the rated maximum, often due to a locked rotor, phase imbalance, or refrigerant system fault.

Unlike E5 which is a software-detected overcurrent, E201 typically indicates that a physical overcurrent relay or CT (current transformer) has tripped. This suggests a more severe condition.

The compressor may be trying to start against high head pressure (short cycling without adequate equalization time) or may have internal winding damage. Electrical supply issues such as phase loss or low voltage are also common causes.

causes.cause: High head pressure from dirty condenser or overcharge
Low supply voltage or phase imbalance
Failed inverter board sending incorrect frequency to compressor

symptoms: E201 displayed on control panel
Outdoor unit shuts down completely
Clicking sound from contactor but compressor does not start
Circuit breaker may trip repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Wait and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit at the breaker and wait at least 10 minutes for pressures to equalize before attempting restart. Then power on.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coil
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the outdoor coil thoroughly with a garden hose. Blocked coils raise head pressure which increases compressor current draw.

fixSteps.title: Check voltage supply
fixSteps.instruction: Measure supply voltage at the outdoor disconnect. For single-phase units, verify 220-240V. For three-phase, verify all three phases are present and balanced within 2%.

fixSteps.title: Professional compressor diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the error recurs, a technician must test compressor winding resistance, insulation resistance to ground, and check the inverter board output.

whenToCallPro: E201 often indicates a serious compressor or electrical fault. If a simple reset and coil cleaning do not resolve it, call an HVAC professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E201 compressor overcurrent fix

faq.q: Is E201 a serious error?
faq.a: Yes. Overcurrent protection tripping repeatedly can damage the compressor. Do not ignore this error.

faq.q: Can I fix E201 myself?
faq.a: You can try a reset and condenser cleaning. If those do not work, professional service is required for compressor and electrical diagnosis.

title: Compressor Startup Failure
shortDescription: The compressor failed to start within the expected time after receiving a run command.
fullDescription:
Error code E202 indicates that the compressor did not start within the timeout period after the outdoor control board issued a start command. The board monitors compressor current draw and RPM feedback; if neither are detected within the startup window, E202 is generated.

This is different from overcurrent errors — in E202 the compressor simply does not respond. The inverter board may be functioning correctly but the compressor itself may be seized, or there may be a wiring break between the inverter and the compressor.

A single occurrence after a power outage may self-resolve. Repeated E202 errors indicate a compressor or inverter board failure.

causes.cause: Broken wiring between inverter and compressor

symptoms: E202 error code displayed
Outdoor unit is silent — compressor does not start
Fan may run but compressor does not engage
No cooling or heating output

fixSteps.title: Power reset with equalization time
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both indoor and outdoor unit breakers. Wait 15 minutes for refrigerant pressures to equalize, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check for obvious wiring issues
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable and qualified, open the outdoor unit electrical compartment and check for burned wires, loose terminals, or signs of arcing at the compressor connection points.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: A technician needs to test compressor winding resistance (phase-to-phase and phase-to-ground), test the inverter board output, and check the compressor start capacitor if applicable.

whenToCallPro: If a power reset does not resolve E202, call a professional. This error usually requires compressor or inverter board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E202 compressor not starting fix

faq.q: Does E202 mean I need a new compressor?
faq.a: Not necessarily. The inverter board is another common cause. A technician can determine which component has failed.

faq.q: How much does a compressor replacement cost?
faq.a: Samsung AC compressor replacement typically costs $500–$1500 including parts and labor, depending on the unit size.

title: Discharge Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has malfunctioned or is reading out of range.
fullDescription:
Error code E301 indicates a problem with the compressor discharge temperature sensor located on the outdoor unit. This sensor monitors the temperature of the hot refrigerant gas leaving the compressor. It is critical for protecting the compressor from overheating.

When this sensor fails, the control board cannot verify that compressor discharge temperatures are within safe limits. To protect the compressor, the system shuts down. The sensor may have failed electrically (open or short circuit) or may have physically detached from the discharge pipe.

This sensor is typically clamped to the compressor discharge pipe with thermal paste or insulation to ensure accurate readings.
symptoms: E301 error displayed
Compressor stops running
System will not cool or heat
Error appears after unit runs for a period

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the outdoor unit breaker for 30 seconds and restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor attachment
fixSteps.instruction: Open the outdoor unit and locate the discharge temperature sensor clamped to the compressor discharge pipe (the hot pipe). Ensure it is firmly in contact with the pipe.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. At room temperature (25°C) it should read approximately 10–15 kOhms depending on the model. An open or zero reading means the sensor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a replacement sensor, ensuring good thermal contact with the discharge pipe. Use the original clamp and add thermal paste if the original had it.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests normal but the discharge temperature is genuinely high, there may be a refrigerant system problem requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E301 discharge temperature sensor fix

faq.q: What happens if the compressor discharge gets too hot?
faq.a: Excessive discharge temperature can break down the refrigerant oil and damage the compressor. The E301 sensor prevents this.

faq.q: Can I use a generic thermistor?
faq.a: Only if it has the exact same resistance curve as the original Samsung part. Using the wrong thermistor can cause incorrect temperature readings.

title: High Pressure Protection Activated
shortDescription: The refrigerant system high pressure switch has tripped, shutting down the compressor to prevent damage.
fullDescription:
Error code E401 indicates that the high-pressure protection switch on the refrigerant system has been activated. This switch trips when the discharge pressure exceeds the safe limit, typically around 400–430 PSI depending on the model and refrigerant type.

High discharge pressure can result from a dirty or blocked condenser coil, a failed outdoor fan motor (no airflow over the condenser), refrigerant overcharge, or non-condensable gases in the system. In extreme ambient temperatures, an undersized or obstructed unit may also trip the high-pressure switch.

This is a safety-critical protection. The compressor must be shut down when high-pressure conditions exist to prevent catastrophic failure of the compressor or refrigerant lines.

causes.cause: Outdoor fan motor failure — no airflow over condenser
Restriction in the liquid line or expansion device

symptoms: E401 error on display
Compressor shuts off abruptly
Outdoor unit may be very hot to the touch
Outdoor fan may or may not be running

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor fan
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outdoor fan is spinning. If it is not, the condenser cannot reject heat, causing high pressure. A failed fan motor needs professional replacement.

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser coil
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit and clean the outdoor coil with a garden hose. Remove all dirt, cottonwood fluff, leaves, and debris from the coil fins.

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure there are no fences, walls, or plants within 60 cm of the outdoor unit blocking airflow.

fixSteps.title: Wait and reset
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, wait 15 minutes for the high-pressure switch to auto-reset and pressures to equalize. Then restart the unit.

fixSteps.title: Professional check
fixSteps.instruction: If E401 recurs after cleaning and the fan is working, a technician must check refrigerant charge and look for system restrictions.

whenToCallPro: If the condenser is clean and the fan is working but E401 keeps recurring, there is likely a refrigerant issue that requires a licensed HVAC technician.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E401 high pressure error fix

faq.q: Is high pressure dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Excessive refrigerant pressure can cause pipe rupture or compressor failure. The E401 protection prevents this by shutting down the system.

faq.q: Can I manually reset the high-pressure switch?
faq.a: Most Samsung units use an auto-reset high-pressure switch. It resets once pressure drops below the threshold. Wait 15 minutes after turning off the unit.

title: Compressor Discharge Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature has exceeded the maximum safe limit.
fullDescription:
Error code E416 is triggered when the compressor discharge temperature exceeds the maximum safe threshold, typically around 115–120°C. Unlike E301 which indicates a sensor malfunction, E416 means the sensor is reading correctly and the temperature is genuinely too high.

Excessive discharge temperature is usually caused by low refrigerant charge (the compressor superheats the remaining gas excessively), a restricted metering device, or a failing compressor with leaking internal valves. Running the system in extreme ambient heat with a dirty condenser can also push discharge temperatures over the limit.

This is a compressor protection error. Continued operation with high discharge temperatures breaks down the compressor oil, damages valve plates, and can lead to catastrophic compressor failure.

causes.cause: Low refrigerant charge causing high superheat
Restricted expansion valve or capillary tube
Dirty condenser coil causing high head pressure

symptoms: E416 error on display
Compressor shuts down after running for a period
Outdoor unit discharge pipe extremely hot
Reduced cooling capacity before shutdown

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coil
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the outdoor coil thoroughly and clear airflow obstructions. High head pressure contributes to high discharge temperature.

fixSteps.title: Check for refrigerant leak signs
fixSteps.instruction: Look for oil stains around the outdoor unit refrigerant connections, which indicate a leak. Low charge is the most common cause of E416.

fixSteps.title: Professional refrigerant diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: A technician must measure suction and discharge pressures, check superheat, and perform a leak test if charge is low. Do not simply add refrigerant without finding and fixing the leak.

whenToCallPro: E416 almost always requires professional service because it typically involves refrigerant system issues that require gauges and EPA certification.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E416 high discharge temperature fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E301 and E416?
faq.a: E301 means the discharge temperature sensor is faulty. E416 means the sensor is working correctly but the actual temperature is too high.

faq.q: Can running the AC on a very hot day cause E416?
faq.a: Extreme heat combined with a dirty condenser or marginal refrigerant charge can cause E416. The unit should handle rated ambient temperatures if properly maintained.

title: Inverter Compressor DC Link Overvoltage
shortDescription: The DC bus voltage in the inverter module has exceeded the safe operating range.
fullDescription:
Error code E464 indicates that the DC link voltage inside the inverter module has risen above the safe threshold. The inverter converts incoming AC power to DC, then back to variable-frequency AC to drive the compressor. The DC link (bus) voltage must stay within a specific range for safe operation.

Overvoltage on the DC bus can be caused by a power supply voltage spike, a regenerative event when the compressor decelerates rapidly, or a failing inverter board component such as a capacitor or rectifier. Power quality issues in the area, such as voltage surges during grid switching, can also trigger this error.

This is a protective shutdown to prevent damage to the inverter module's power transistors (IGBTs). Repeated E464 events without resolution can destroy the inverter board.

causes.cause: Failing DC bus capacitor on inverter board
Unstable grid voltage in the area

symptoms: E464 error code on display
Outdoor unit shuts down abruptly
Error may coincide with power grid disturbances
May occur during compressor speed changes

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the outdoor unit breaker for 60 seconds to discharge the DC bus capacitors, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Monitor the supply voltage with a multimeter over a period. Look for voltage spikes above 260V (for 230V systems). Consider installing a voltage stabilizer if surges are frequent.

fixSteps.title: Install a surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: If power quality is poor in your area, install a whole-house or dedicated AC surge protector at the disconnect.

fixSteps.title: Professional inverter board check
fixSteps.instruction: If E464 keeps occurring despite stable supply voltage, the inverter board or its DC bus capacitors may be failing and need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If E464 recurs despite stable power supply, the inverter board needs professional testing and likely replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung air conditioner E464 inverter overvoltage error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge damage my Samsung AC permanently?
faq.a: Yes. Voltage spikes can destroy the inverter board's IGBT transistors. A surge protector is strongly recommended.

faq.q: How much does an inverter board replacement cost?
faq.a: Samsung AC inverter boards typically cost $150–$400 for the part alone, plus $100–$200 in labor.
